mechanism_narrative: A rising Industrial Production Index signals expanding manufacturing and utilities output, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings for S&P 500 firms, generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show zero or negative rank correlation with month-over-month changes in INDPRO over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor demand and falling household income, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of an elevated Federal Funds rate and decelerating consumer price inflation indicates a restrictive real monetary stance that compresses equity valuation multiples and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A composite z-score interaction of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative S&P 500 20-day returns with Rank IC magnitude below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth during periods of strong consumer spending-led expansion directly lifts top-line revenues for consumer-facing S&P 500 companies, generating positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 45% of weeks during consumer-led expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows dominates earnings growth dynamics, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in retail sales and payroll employment captures a coordinated expansion in both consumer demand and labor income, creating a durable macroeconomic tailwind that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of contemporaneous positive changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to generate positive S&P 500 60-day returns with a hit rate above 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in Industrial Production exceeding 1.0% over two consecutive months signals a contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% over 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects the aggregate output and income expansion of the U.S. economy, providing a fundamental anchor for S&P 500 corporate earnings and generating positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter changes in GDPC1 show zero or negative rank correlation with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.
